title: Batesian Mimicry Explained
script_summary: A concise exploration of Batesian mimicry and its implications in nature.

segments.voiceover: Some creatures deceive to survive. How?
Meet Batesian mimicry, where harmless species imitate dangerous ones.
This strategy confuses predators, increasing survival chances.
Take the viceroy butterfly; it resembles the toxic monarch, tricking birds.
But is all mimicry effective? Not always—environment plays a key role.
Want more mind-bending nature facts? Hit follow!
